title: Eva Unit 01 Deus ex machina
wikitext:
[]: Eva Unit 01: Deus ex machina?             Quote Post by Cross of Iron » Tue Jun 14, 2011 11:59 am Just another random Eva-related thought I had. This one pertaining to the scene in EoE where Eva Unit 01 fuses with the Tree of Life and turns into that giant red tree-thing, and how Fuyutsuki says that the eva has gained the fruits of knowledge and wisdom. Any thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jun 14, 2011 12:09 pm Is this thread about Eva-01 turning into a god, or Eva-01 being a literary deux ex machina ?

[]:             Quote Post by Cross of Iron » Tue Jun 14, 2011 12:19 pm It's where Eva Unit 01 turns into a god in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Jun 14, 2011 1:01 pm Eva-01 is pretty much deux ex machina yeah, it's hax powers are always used to explain shit that doesn't make any sense (even after the explanation) and it's used to resolve narrative whenever ITSA PIIINCH

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Jun 14, 2011 6:36 pm Cross of Iron wrote: It's where Eva Unit 01 turns into a god in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Jun 14, 2011 7:21 pm Yeah, Deus ex Machina usually refers to "The Hand of God", where good pulls out in the end despite it making no sense whatsoever. Usually it's a technique that a writer turns to because he's written himself in a corner, not because he was building up and preparing to use it. So I've seen instances where it is used very well, where the writer built up to that event emotionally, including Evangelion, and instances where you can tell the writer was just trying to get himself out of a narrative jam. But, yes, Eva Unit 01 does turn into a god in EoE. So I guess that does put a new spin on the meaning "The Hand of God". It also suggests that Anno was building to that moment rather than writing himself out of a corner. But you can also tell this was the case from the other credible writing techniques that build up to "things happening that make no sense." That sense of mystery and wonder is an important part of the Evangelion narrative, so techniques like this are quite well adaptable to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Jun 14, 2011 9:49 pm "deus ex machina" was initially used as a stage term to refer to Greek drama, wherein a writer who had written himself into a corner would just have an actor playing a god descend from on high and resolve the plot. But I don't think Unit 01 counts as a deus ex machina in this sense, because she and her powers don't appear out of nowhere and they don't resolve the plot, they're at fault for it. The whole story is on some level about Yui's apotheosis and how it wrecks more or less everything, although it isn't without some positive consequences. Her abilities are hax, but there's a difference between that and full-on deus ex machina. If you want an even more literal deus ex machina, try the Lance of Longinus that hits Unit 02 in the face. However, "deus ex machina" can also refer to the older sense of machine/mechanism, a mechanical creation, something made by human agency and work. In that sense, Gendou is trying to cast Yui as a deus ex machina: he is, by his own endeavor, creating a god which will enable him to resolve his scenario. Doesn't work for him, but Yui does cast herself as a deus ex machina too: only her scenario is different. So for Anno's purposes she really isn't a deus ex machina, but "a god made by human effort" is certainly an appropriate description of Unit 01.

[]:             Quote Post by Cross of Iron » Tue Jun 14, 2011 11:22 pm So it's more of a man-made god type of thing. That's actually what I thought all along.
